product_summary.one_sentence_summary: Hands-on prompt engineering workshop for teams already using AI, framed around better output quality, repeatable prompts and internal best practices.
product_summary.target_user: Teams that already use ChatGPT or other LLM tools and need more reliable prompting habits.
product_summary.core_pain: Users can already access AI but their prompts are inconsistent, inefficient and hard to operationalise.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: Better prompt quality, a reusable prompt library and more consistent AI outputs for work tasks.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The workshop is likely bought by a company to improve how staff use AI tools at work.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: HR/L&D can own team learning budgets for this type of workshop.
Innovation, operations or function leads may also co-own the decision because the use case is productivity-driven.

budget_owner.reason: The workshop is most naturally funded by the team or business unit that will use the prompts.

buyer_influencers.reason: Teams exploring AI adoption are likely to be influenced by innovation or transformation stakeholders.
Data-handling and safe-use concerns can block rollout if not addressed.
Prompt use for real work may require legal or privacy review if sensitive data is involved.
HR/L&D may still influence training design and vendor selection.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: House of Training already offers prompt-engineering training in Luxembourg.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: House of Training has a public prompt engineering course for financial-sector employees and broader ChatGPT/GenAI courses that cover prompt creation and practical use.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Free public guidance exists for generic prompt engineering.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: OpenAI publishes free prompting guidance and Academy materials, which weakens any claim that prompt engineering knowledge is scarce or uniquely packaged.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Practical prompt use should be combined with privacy and governance awareness.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The House of Training finance courses explicitly connect prompting with GDPR and AI Act requirements, and OpenAI guidance also assumes careful prompt design and context handling.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Luxembourg company training co-funding is relevant to this product category.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Guichet confirms co-funding for continuing vocational training in Luxembourg, subject to the usual exclusions.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The workshop will deliver a company-specific prompt library of 30-50 tested prompts.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: This is a proposed deliverable, not a public fact.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: Customization is the main differentiator, not knowledge.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: This is a positioning interpretation, not an externally verified fact.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: SME Package AI 70% applies to this workshop.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The reviewed sources do not prove that the exact AI-package claim applies to this product.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The workshop can be sold as a 1-2 day format with a prompt library outcome.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The duration and deliverable set are internal design choices rather than public facts.

regulator_entities.name: European Commission AI Office
regulator_entities.relevance: Provides AI literacy materials and the Article 4 context that makes safe prompt use relevant for employers.

framework_entities.name: AI Act Article 4
framework_entities.relevance: The broader AI literacy and safe-use environment in which this workshop sits.

framework_entities.name: Prompting
framework_entities.relevance: Free public guidance on prompt design and best practices.

funding_bodies.name: INFPC / Guichet.lu
funding_bodies.relevance: Relevant for Luxembourg company training co-funding.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: AI literacy obligations apply to providers and deployers of AI systems.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Organisations using AI systems in the EU

subsidies_or_funding.name: Continuing vocational training co-funding
subsidies_or_funding.eligibility_notes: Available in principle for private-sector training plans, but product-level fit must be confirmed.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: House of Training - Prompt Engineering : Initiation & mise en pratique pour les employés du secteur financier
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: A public Luxembourg prompt-engineering product with compliance framing for finance staff.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: House of Training - Entrepreneur augmenté grâce à ChatGPT
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: A practical ChatGPT course explicitly teaching prompt engineering and operational efficiency.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: OpenAI Academy - Prompting
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Free public prompting guidance substitutes for generic prompting knowledge.

legal_claim_severity.reason: This is a productivity and skills workshop, so the legal claim is soft rather than regulated-practice level.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: Hands-on AI training can be delivered internally, but privacy and governance review is advisable.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about reliable work outputs and safe use benefit from external SME review rather than pure self-assertion.

proof_stack.reason: The market is crowded with prompt-engineering content, so buyer willingness matters first.
A paid pilot will show whether the custom library is actually valued.
Participants should demonstrate better prompt quality and more consistent outputs after training.

proof_burden.reason: Because generic knowledge is freely available, the product must prove customisation and buyer acceptance rather than just attendance.

dependency_types.reason: Prompting on real business tasks can expose sensitive data or documents.
The workshop is Luxembourg-scoped and should use local examples and governance framing.
An SME partner or reviewer helps keep the prompt library credible and safe.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The current scope is a generic workshop for teams, but value will vary by function, data sensitivity and AI maturity.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Expanding to other countries or sectors changes privacy, language and governance expectations substantially.

subsidy_exists.reason: General company training co-funding exists in Luxembourg.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: The general training route exists, but the exact AI-package claim for this workshop is not proven.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: The card should not present the subsidy claim as settled when the exact workshop-level fit is not verified.

market_regulatory_context.reason: Prompt engineering itself is not regulated, but the offer sits next to privacy, AI Act and safe-use concerns.

claim_language_risk.reason: Prompt-library and safe-use language is acceptable, but it should stay clear of overstating formal compliance effects.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: No formal authority is required, but privacy and governance review matter for real-work prompting.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Prompt-engineering content already exists in Luxembourg and free public guidance is available.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The main number-based claims are internal design choices rather than public market facts.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: General co-funding exists, but the exact AI-package claim should not be assumed.

positioning_risk.reason: Without tight customisation, the product can collapse into a generic prompt class that is easy to substitute.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: The market needs this workshop because no comparable prompt-engineering products exist locally.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: House of Training already sells Luxembourg prompt-engineering and ChatGPT courses, and OpenAI provides free public prompting guidance.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Show a concrete customisation layer, not only generic prompt instruction.
Define what kinds of client prompts and documents can be used safely in the workshop.
Confirm whether the workshop belongs under general training co-funding only.
